rulings.Case Title: RODRIGO LOPEZ VS. ALTAS PROPERTY GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Clearly Identify Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT ATLAS PROPERTY GROUP, INC., 1830 ALEMANY, LLC, YOSEF TAHBAZOF Notice Of Motion To Amend Judgment To Clearly Identify Costs is continued to November 17, 2023. No later than October 27, 2023 moving party to provide copies of all the request for an abstract with a corresponding rejection letter. Additionally, moving party to provide a copy of the proposed abstract of judgement addressing ALL items in the rejection letter(s). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DONALD A. PETERSON VS. JEAN I. MENES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees And Cost
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF DONALD PETERSON, DONALD A. PETERSON, AS TRUSTEE OF THE PETERSON Notice And Motion For Award Of Reasonable Attorney Fees And Cost is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the claimed $55,540 in attorney's fees was incurred for the "common benefit" within the meaning of the partition statutes. Costs may be requested by memorandum of costs.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. KINNEY HOTEL, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joint Motion Of Kevin Singer, Receiver, And Plaintiff Umpqua Bank For Order (1) Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale And (2) Confirming Sale Procedures
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF UNITED BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION Notice Of Joint Motion Of Kevin Singer, Receiver, And Plaintiff Umpqua Bank For Order (1) Instructing And Authorizing Receiver To List And Market 410 Eddy Street, San Francisco, California For Sale And (2) Confirming Sale Procedures is DENIED. Plaintiff obtained a final judgment on the sole cause of action for specific performance and appointment of a receiver in this action. Per the terms of the loan documents, the receiver is in place "to take possession of all or any part of the Property, with the power to protect and preserve the Property, to operate the property preceding foreclosure or sale, and to collect the Rents from the Property, and apply the proceeds, over and above the costs of the receivership, against the Indebtedness." Since the relief requested in this action has been obtain in full, Plaintiff shall proceed with its other remedies available under the law or contract with "foreclosure or sale" within the meaning of the contract. The Court will not authorize the receiver to proceed with the sale on a matter reduced to judgement on the relief requested. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of Unitedlayer???S Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 5. CROSS COMPLAINANT UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Under Seal Portions Of Unitedlayer???S Cross-Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Declaration in support of the motion is conclusory and does not support the request as to all portions of the cross-complaint sought to be sealed. rulings.Case Title: SAIJUN LUO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. DOLORES PARK TERRACES OWNER, LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDNATS' NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 6. DEFENDNATS' NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND for Plaintiff to state specific facts, indicating where the mold was (or other conditions P cites, such as vermin, odors, etc.), how Defendants refused to remediate, how Defendants' failure to remediate caused Plaintiff emotional distress, and a description of Plaintiff's emotional distress response.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SAIJUN LUO, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. DOLORES PARK TERRACES OWNER, LLC, A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT DOLORES PARK TERRACES OWNER, LLC, A DELAWARE, GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC., A CALIFORNIA, ZOEY WESTRATE, AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Plaintiff requested attorney fees in the Enforcement of the Contract, but the provision in the Lease for attorney's fees arises in the context of indemnification and not litigation, and thus are not covered by CCP 1717. Further, Plaintiff requested attorney fees under CCP 1179, but because Plaintiff is not seeking relief from forfeiture and has not plead any hardship, attorney fees are not available.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: TURNER BRADSHAW VS. JEAN PAUL Y. ESGUERRA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 12, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT JEAN-PAUL ESGUERRA DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to explain the discrepancy on the "Affidavit of Eligible Bidder" which starts with "I, Turner Bradshaw" and is signed by one "Robert Bradshaw." The Court notes that that in opposition Mr. Bradshaw claims that he uses the name "Robert" for "important" "legal documents." Neither the Complaint (important verified legal document) nor the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale (important legal document) are in the name "Robert." Plaintiff to explain how the choice of name is made (Turner vs. Robert) in the Amended Complaint if Plaintiff amends the complaint in the manner consistent with the explanation provided in the opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
